name: Abyssal Anglerfish
ability: 3 [Wave] if no tokens on this fish
description: Unlike some anglerfishes, the tiny males of this deepsea blackdevil are not believed to be parasitic.

name: Abyssal Halosaur
description: It often hovers just above the seafloor, pectoral fins held erect and pointing forward, possibly serving a sensory function.

name: Abyssal Spiderfish
description: Underwater images have shown the trails made when this fish “walks” over the seafloor on its pelvic and tail fins.

name: Angelshark
ability: 10 [Wave] if [ConsumeFish3] (there are 3+ fish under this one)
description: The angelshark is an ambush predator, camouflaging itself on the sea floor until prey gets close.

name: Angler
description: The angler uses a special dorsal fin ray to lure passing fish near its mouth, gulping them down.

name: Arabian Carpetshark
description: This hardy shark inhabits coastal waters that feature coral reefs, lagoons, rocky shores, and mangrove estuaries.

name: Atlantic Bluefin Tuna
description: The bluefin tuna is one of the open ocean’s fastest and strongest predators, reaching speeds of up to 69 km/hr.

name: Atlantic Bonito
ability: 6 [Wave] if [ConsumeFish2] (there are 2+ fish under this one)
description: The Atlantic bonito uses ram ventilation, meaning that it must keep swimming to breathe. It swallows prey whole.

name: Atlantic Mackerel
description: Unlike other mackerel, this fish does not leap out of the water unless it’s attempting to escape a predator.

name: Atlantic Sailfish
description: The Atlantic sailfish hunts schooling fish, such as sardines, anchovies, and mackerel, utilizing its pointed bill.

name: Atlantic Salmon
description: Atlantic salmon go out to sea to grow. Then they return to the river where they were born to spawn.

name: Atlantic Sturgeon
description: Its eggs are prized as high-quality caviar, and now it is threatened by overfishing, pollution, and habitat loss.

name: Atlantic Wolffish
description: Males of this species guard new eggs, hardly feeding as they do. Their bite can crush the hard shells of mollusks.

name: Banggai Cardinalfish
description: Due to limited geographical diversity and its popularity in the aquarium trade, this fish has become endangered.

name: Barramundi
description: Virtually all barramundi are born male, then turn female when they are three to four years old.

name: Bearded Seadevil
description: Bearded seadevils attract prey with their bioluminescent lure and elaborate bioluminescent chin barbel.

name: Bigeye Tuna
description: This fish dives deep during the day and returns to surface waters by nightfall, a 20ºC difference in temperature.

name: Bigeye Smooth-Head
description: The genus was first discovered in 1911 off the coast of the Baja California peninsula, where its Latin name comes from.

name: Binocular Fish
description: Binocular fish have highly developed tubular eyes that face forward in their transparent heads.

name: Black Cardinalfish
description: Found in most temperate oceans, this species is believed to have a lifespan of over 100 years.

name: Black Hagfish
description: Hagfish produce copious amounts of expanding slime that can fill even the largest predator’s mouth and gills.

name: Black Swallower
description: It can swallow prey up to ten times its size, coiling the other fish inside its distended stomach.

name: Blob Sculpin
description: Named the world’s ugliest animal in 2013, the “blobfish” soared to fame through memes, songs, toys, and cartoons.

name: Blue Tang
description: When in danger, it raises a pair of venomous spines on either side of its tail, then whips its body back and forth.

name: Bluespine Unicornfish
description: The horn-like appendage between its eyes starts growing when the fish reaches about 13 cm in length.

name: Bluestreak Cleaner Wrasse
description: These fish set up cleaning stations to remove and eat unwanted ectoparasites on “client” fish.

name: Bluntnose Sixgill Shark
description: One of the world’s largest sharks, it appears similar to fossilized remains dating back roughly 200 million years

name: Chinese Trumpetfish
description: This solitary fish often darts down vertically to hunt, using suction to draw prey into its elongated, tubular snout.

name: Chola Guitarfish
description: Found on shallow, soft bottoms, this fish gets its common name from its guitar-like shape.

name: Clown Anemonefish
description: Living within an anemone in a symbiotic relationship, the two creatures defend each other from predators.

name: Common Fangtooth
description: They have opposing sockets on either side of their brain to accommodate their fangs when their mouth is closed.

name: Cookiecutter Shark
ability: 5 [Wave] if [PlayFishBottomRow] (this fish is in the bottom row)
description: It is called a cookiecutter shark because it takes a perfectly round, cookie-sized plug of flesh from its prey.

name: Cownose Ray
description: Its common name comes from the shape of its head, which resembles a cow nose when seen from above.

name: Creolefish
description: A relatively short snout gives it binocular vision. When it sees danger, it retreats rapidly into rocks or a reef.

name: Crocodilefish
description: Its eyes have iris lappets, pale projections that resemble eyelashes and provide camouflage for the black pupils.

name: Deepsea Lizardfish
description: It rests on the seafloor, head slightly elevated, ready to strike any prey that comes close—even its own kind.

name: Diamond Stingray
description: Common near beaches, it’s not aggressive, but its long, venomous tail spine can still be dangerous to people.

name: Elsman's Whipnose
description: It has a long whip-like lure attached to the tip of its snout, and approximately five rows of needle-like teeth.

name: Emperor Red Snapper
description: It is a snapper, not an emperor fish. Juveniles will often shelter among sea urchin spines for protection.

name: Escolar
description: The flesh of this fish is very oily, which makes it delicious to humans but dangerous to consume in large quantities.

name: European Anchovy
description: It is euryhaline, meaning it can live in water with low salinity, such as lagoons, estuaries, and brackish lakes.

name: European Flounder
description: The flounder can be found in the sea, in estuaries, and even sometimes in freshwater rivers.

name: European Sturgeon
description: Once abundant and found in many coastal habitats around Europe, it is now a critically endangered species.

name: Faceless Cusk
description: First collected by the HMS Challenger in 1878, the so-called “faceless fish” was not rediscovered until 2017.

name: False Catshark
description: Even though it has long, narrow, catlike eyes, it is not a catshark. It’s much larger and has hundreds of tiny teeth.

name: False Cleanerfish
description: It mimics the cleaner wrasse and then takes a bite out of the unexpecting fish waiting for a cleaning.

name: Flabby Whalefish
description: The tiny males, once believed to be members of a different family, look quite different than the females and do not feed as adults.

name: Footballfish
description: The modified ray on its head makes a thick fishing rod, tipped with a luminous bulb, used to lure prey.

name: Giant Devil Ray
description: Devil rays get their common name from the twin horns on their head that are actually fins used to help them feed.

name: Giant Hatchetfish
description: Through the use of bioluminescence, this fish can both make itself appear invisible and find other fish more easily.

name: Giant Hawkfish
description: Named for its hawklike hunting technique, it perches on the high point of a coral reef and dives down upon prey.

name: Giant Manta Ray
description: The largest ray in the world, it has the biggest brain of any fish by volume—ten times larger than a whale shark’s.

name: Giant Oarfish
description: Rarely seen, the oarfish is the world’s longest bony fish. It’s believed to be the cause of sea serpent sightings.

name: Giant Trevally
description: The giant trevally is known to prey upon fledgling seabirds that fall into the water while learning to fly.

name: Gray Triggerfish
description: Its first, tall dorsal spine remains erect until the smaller second spine is deflexed, triggering the first.

name: Great Northern Tilefish
description: This colorful fish is known as the clown of the sea. It burrows into the sediment at the bottom of the ocean.

name: Great White Shark
description: Known by scientists as simply the “white shark,” this famous predator is, itself, occasionally preyed upon by orca whales.

name: Greenland Shark
description: This shark currently holds the record as the longest-lived vertebrate, with an estimated lifespan of over 250 years.

name: Grideye Fish
description: Its eyes are flat, cornea-like, light-sensitive organs without lenses that cover most of the upper surface of the head.

name: Haddock
description: It lives on gravelly, sandy, and pebbly seafloors, where it feeds on small fish, worms, mollusks, and eggs.

name: Hogfish
description: Its common name comes from its long pig-like snout and how it roots around the seafloor searching for food.

name: Honeycomb Scaly Dragonfish
description: This fish can be found in deep waters during the day and in shallower areas at night, where it searches for food.

name: Humpback Anglerfish
description: The humpback anglerfish is more commonly found at shallower depths than other species in its genus.

name: Humphead Wrasse
description: It excavates by ejecting water to displace sand. Then it noses around for food.

name: Indo-Pacific Sailfish
description: The world’s fastest swimming fish, its intricate dorsal fin, shaped like a sail, helps it to reach up to 110 km per hour.

name: Japanese Anchovy
description: Large schools tend to swim close to the surface during the full moon, their silver scales shimmering in the light.

name: Kaluga
description: Endangered throughout its range from overfishing, this sturgeon spends much of its life in estuaries and rivers.

name: Kawakawa
description: Adults live in open waters but remain close to the shoreline. Their young may enter bays and harbors.

name: Largetooth Flounder
description: The largetooth flounder is a species of left- eyed flatfish with both eyes of the adult on the left side of its body.

name: Leafy Seadragon
description: Lobes of skin growing all over it provide camouflage, giving it the appearance of seaweed, even as it swims.

name: Little Skate
description: Its tail contains an organ that intermittently generates a weak electric field that may be used for communication.

name: Live Sharksucker
description: This fish can attach itself to any large host, even scuba divers, using the oval-shaped sucking disc on its head.

name: Long-Snouted Lancetfish
description: Voracious eaters, many new species of fish and mollusks have been discovered in the contents of their stomachs.

name: Longspine Porcupinefish
description: It uses its beak and the plates on the roof of its mouth to crush prey that would otherwise be indigestible.

name: Mahi-Mahi
description: Its common name means “strong-strong” in Hawaiian. Flyingfishes make up nearly 25% of the diet of adults.

name: Maletese Ray
description: Critically endangered due to habitat loss, the Maltese ray faces a high risk of extinction in the wild.

name: Mariana Snailfish
description: It has been observed at a depth of 8,178 m, making it one of the deepest dwelling fishes on the planet.

name: Mediterranean Damselfish
description: Though normally a peaceful species, males aggressively guard the eggs while they develop.

name: Mediterranean Parrotfish
description: It starts life as female, then changes to male in adulthood, turning from bright red and yellow to gray and blue.

name: Midwater Scorpionfish
description: This fish has often been seen suspending itself vertically in the water, with its mouth pointing up towards the surface.

name: Ocean sunfish
description: The largest known bony fish, it basks on the surface to thermally recharge after feeding in deeper, colder waters.

name: Oceanic Puffer
description: Its Latin name means “rabbit head,” and, like many puffers, it contains neurotoxins that may be fatal to humans if eaten.

name: Pacific Sardine
description: Forming schools of up to 10 million fish, it is, at times, the most abundant species in the California Current.

name: Pacific White Skate
description: Most skates only have dermal denticles on their dorsal side, but this one has tiny spines on its belly as well.

name: Pale Chimaera
description: Dubbed “ghost sharks,” chimaeras “flap” their large pectoral fins to propel themselves forward (unlike sharks).

name: Paintspotted Moray
description: It inhabits reef flats and rocky intertidal shorelines, and it will sometimes leave the water in pursuit of prey.

name: Paraliparis
description: This species does not have a common name of its own. It’s a snailfish—scaleless with a thin, loose, gelatinous skin.

name: Patagonian Toothfish
description: Antifreeze proteins prevent its blood and tissues from freezing in subzero temperatures.

name: Pelican Eel
description: The mouth of the pelican eel can open wide enough to swallow prey much larger than the eel itself.

name: Pennant Coralfish
description: The juveniles are solitary and sometimes clean parasites off other, larger fish. Adults live in pairs and feed on zooplankton.

name: Pilotfish
description: This carnivorous fish tends to follow sharks and ships to feed on parasites and leftover scraps of food.

name: Plate Fish
description: Plate fish are experts at camouflage and can quickly change colors to match their surroundings.

name: Pudgy Cusk-Eel
description: The pudgy cusk-eel is a common fish of deep oceans. It lives along the ocean floor as deep as 4,300 m.

name: Pygmy Seahorse
description: It is nearly indistinguishable from the coral where it is found because of its tiny size and specialized camouflage.

name: Red Lionfish
description: Though it is a staple in the aquarium fish trade and a popular attraction for divers, this fish is quite venomous.

name: Red Scorpionfish
description: A lurking fish that uses camouflage to surprise its food, the spines of its dorsal fin house potent venom glands.

name: Rockmover Wrasse
description: To expose its prey, it will overturn rocks. If threatened, it will dive into the sand and wriggle its way to safety.

name: Sailfin Flying Fish
description: When they leave the water, they can fly up to 400 meters at speeds of up to 70 km per hour.

name: Sargassum Fish
description: Sargassum fish spend most of their life drifting with the floating seaweed mats where they live and hunt.

name: Scaleless Black Dragonfish
description: It has a fleshy bioluminescent barbel at its chin, used to lure prey within reach of its heavily toothed mouth.

name: Shortspine African Angler
description: The color of its body changes to match its surroundings. It can fully swallow prey as large as itself.

name: Shortspine Spurdog
description: A type of dogfish found along the continental shelves, it gives birth to live young after a two-year gestation period.

name: Silver Lightfish
description: Rows of violet light organs run along its belly, and each eye has additional light organs in front and behind it.

name: Skipjack Tuna
description: Skipjack makes up about 70% of all canned or pouched tuna. It is the most caught tuna in the world.

name: Sloan's Viperfish
description: Its jaw can be unhinged, allowing it to open its mouth up to 90 degrees to catch prey as large as 63% of its own body size.

name: Smooth Oreo Dory
description: The smooth oreo dory inhabits continental slopes of all southern continents at depths up to 1,500 m.

name: Snaggletooth
description: It has rows of photophores on its flanks and luminescent patches above its eyes, nostrils, and pectoral fin.

name: Snipe Eel
description: The snipe eel’s most unusual feature is its long, slender jaws that curve at the end like the bill of a snipe (bird).

name: Speckled Butterflyfish
description: It can easily swim sideways or upside down. One of the few fish that mates for life, it often swims in mated pairs.

name: Spiny Seadevil
description: The tiny, parasitic male attaches itself to the larger female, its tissues and blood vessels fusing with its host.

name: Spookfish
description: There are several known species of spookfish, an elongated type of barreleye. This species is known from only a few specimens.

name: Spotted Parrotfish
description: With teeth hard as metal, parrotfish eat coral and excrete sand. They also sleep in a protective cocoon of mucus.

name: Stonefish
description: The world’s most venomous fish, if you mistake it for a harmless rock, it will pop up its dorsal spines and sting.

name: Stoplight Loosejaw
description: Its jaw is much longer than its skull, nearly 30% of its body length, and its mouth has no floor membrane.

name: Striped Marlin
description: Compared to other marlin species, the bill of a striped marlin is a bit more slender and blunt.

name: Swordfish
description: Among the fastest of fish, the swordfish uses its bill to slash at its prey to make for an easier catch.

name: Tripodfish
description: Perching on the ocean floor on three long, modified fin rays, it faces into the current to intercept prey drifting by.

name: Tropical Two-Wing Flying Fish
description: The exceptionally large pectoral fins enable this fish to glide for long distances above the surface of the water.

name: Whale Shark
description: The largest fish in the world, it may live 130 years. It has over 300 teeth but feeds on tiny fish, shrimp, and plankton.

name: White Suckerfish
description: It usually attaches itself to a large ray, but sometimes to a shark or a marlin. It often enters the gill chamber or mouth of its host.

name: White-Spotted Puffer
description: Also known as the stars-and-stripes puffer, its skin and internal organs are poisonous to humans.

name: Whitemargin Stargazer
description: This venomous fish has electric organs located in a pouch behind its eyes and can discharge up to 50 volts.

name: Wolftrap Angler
description: This fish has its own natural fishing rod, complete with fishing line, fishhooks, and bioluminescent bait.

name: Yellow Boxfish
description: When it’s threatened, the yellow boxfish releases toxins that protect it from predators.

name: Yellowtail Snapper
description: Its Latin name is derived from the words “golden swift tail” because its bright yellow tail is forked like that of a swift.

name: Barreleye
description: Its head is transparent with tubular eyes peering up from inside. The twin eyeholes in front are actually nostrils.

name: Blue Lanternfish
description: Using glowing blue-green lights embedded all over its body, it can see and signal in the dark ocean depths.

name: Bonefish
description: While feeding, it can be found in waters as shallow as 10 cm. Then it retreats to waters as deep as 100 meters.

name: Smoothcheek Lanternfish
description: These bioluminescent fish are found in the depths during the day and rise to the surface at night to feed.

name: Longfin Batfish
description: A peaceful, social fish, the longfin batfish forms schools with fish from other species.

name: Mandarinfish
description: Its common name comes from its vivid coloration, evoking the robes of the imperial Chinese civil service.

name: Orange Roughy
description: Orange roughy are estimated to live for over 150 years and don’t reproduce until they are 20 to 40 years old.

name: Porkfish
description: It gets its common name from the sound it makes when it grinds its teeth and how it makes pig-like grunts.

name: Saddleback Butterflyfish
description: “Saddleback” refers to its white-bordered black spot. This fish inhabits coral reefs, rocky shores, and lagoons.

name: White-Banded Triggerfish
description: Its body is divided into roughly geometric areas, each with a different design and color pattern.

name: American Pocket Shark
description: Besides clusters of photophores on its body, it has two pockets near its gills that secrete a luminous fluid.

name: Angular Roughshark
description: It has a wide pink snout, massive nostrils, and pronounced dermal denticles, giving it a furry, pig-like appearance.

name: Armored Searobin
ability: [Discard] also, if [BlueCoral][BlueCoral][BlueCoral] in this dive site: [DrawCard]
description: The armored searobin's body is completely covered in a heavy plating composed of four rows of bony spiny scales.

name: Atlantic Thornyhead
ability: [FishHatch] also, if [GreenCoral][GreenCoral][GreenCoral] in this dive site: [FishEgg]
description: The Atlantic thornyhead has a spined ridge between its eyes as well as spines along its back.

name: Basking Shark
description: A filter feeder and the second-largest known living fish, the basking shark can be found in all temperate oceans.

name: Blackmouth Angler
ability: [FreePlayFishFromHand] if no [AnyCoral] in this fish's dive site
description: The blackmouth angler hides in the mud or sand and uses its fleshy lure to ambush prey.

name: Blacktip Shark
description: A fast, active swimmer, it sometimes makes spinning leaps out of the water when hunting schooling fishes.

name: Blue Shark
description: Female blue sharks birth from 4 to over 100 pups per litter, each 35 to 50 cm in length, in nursery areas found offshore.

name: Bluering Angelfish
ability: [FishEgg] also, if [PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral] in this dive site: [ConsumeFish1]
description: The bluering angelfish's Latin name means “ringed,” which refers to the small ring on its body above the gill cover.

name: Bonnethead Shark
description: The only shark species known to be omnivorous, its diet includes crustaceans, mollusks, small fish, and seagrass.

name: Broadnose Sevengill Shark
description: Alongside the sharpnose sevengill (Heptranchias perlo), it's listed in the Guinness World Records as the fish with the most gill slits.

name: Common Bluestripe Snapper
description: It has a varied diet, feeding on fishes, shrimps, crabs, and crustaceans, as well as plant and algae material.

name: Daggertooth
ability: [FishHatch] also, if [GreenCoral][GreenCoral][GreenCoral] in this dive site: [Discard]
description: Older daggertooth appear to turn black, lose their teeth, and migrate to deeper waters, possibly to reproduce.

name: Dusky Shark
description: It migrates hundreds to thousands of km to temperate waters in the summer and toward the equator in the winter.

name: Dwarf Lanternshark
description: One of the smallest known sharks, the dwarf lanternshark's bioluminescent belly helps it blend in with sunlight from above.

name: Epaulette Shark
description: It can swim, but it mostly “walks” along coral reefs on its fins. It can even travel short distances over land.

name: Filetail Catshark
description: This catshark's common name comes from the toothlike projections on its skin.

name: Fire Dartfish
description: It usually hovers above its burrow, quick to dart inside. It flicks its long dorsal fin back and forth, perhaps as a signal.

name: Flame Angelfish
description: The male fish are slightly larger than the females, and the blue streaks along the fins are more apparent.

name: Foxface Rabbitfish
description: During nighttime or when stressed, the foxface rabbitfish changes to a duller, mottled pattern that's harder to see.

name: Frilled Shark
description: It moves like an eel, capturing prey whole with flexible jaws that are lined with bunches of recurved trident-like teeth.

name: Furry Coffinfish
ability: [Discard] also, if [BlueCoral][BlueCoral][BlueCoral] in this dive site: [SchoolFeederMove]
description: It can increase its size up to 30% by inflating its body with water and holding its breath up to four minutes.

name: Ghostly Seadevil
ability: [FishEgg] also, if [PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral] in this dive site: [SchoolFeederMove]
description: Instead of a lure, this translucent anglerfish uses a small bioluminescent skin flap to attract prey.

name: Giant Moray
description: Sheltering by day in crevices up to  50 meters deep, this nocturnal ambush predator hunts primarily by smell.

name: Goblin Shark
description: A sluggish swimmer, it can dramatically extend its specialized protrusible jaws to quickly capture nearby prey.

name: Great Barracuda
description: Its long, prominent teeth fit into pockets on the opposing jaw, allowing it to close its mouth, locking onto its prey.

name: Great Hammerhead
description: Its unmistakably shaped head provides stereoscopic vision and improves electromagnetic signal detection.

name: Gray Reef Shark
description: Fast, agile, and social, they form schools near coral reef drop-offs, but these groups disperse at night.

name: Gulper Shark
description: A slender dogfish with two spined dorsal fins and large, green eyes, it typically hunts along the ocean's bottom.

name: Indian Sail-fin Surgeonfish
description: At night its skin becomes paler and more uniform in response to the darkness, helping it to pass unnoticed.

name: Jensen's Skate
ability: [Discard] also, if [BlueCoral][BlueCoral][BlueCoral] in this dive site: [FishHatch]
description: Named in honor of Danish zoologist, Adolf Severin Jensen, this skate has been found at depths over 2,500 meters.

name: Kitefin Shark
description: The largest known shark that exhibits bioluminescence, it hunts mostly deep-sea fishes, including other sharks and rays.

name: Lemon Shark
description: This sociable shark's brain has the relative mass of a bird's. Its pups may remain for years in the same nursery grounds.

name: Leopard Shark
description: Common in shallow, intertidal waters and kelp forests, the leopard shark often forages in large nomadic schools.

name: Lollipop Catshark
description: Its body is soft, almost gelatinous. Its head contains unusually large gills, allowing it to survive in low-oxygen habitats.

name: Longnose Hawkfish
description: It's often seen perched, camouflaged, on a red gorgonian sea fan, waiting to make a short, fast rush at its prey.

name: Malabar Grouper
description: Found in tropical estuaries and around coastal coral and rocky reefs, it preys on fish, crustaceans, and occasionally cephalopods.

name: Megamouth Shark
description: Only around 200 specimens have been observed or caught. It swims with its mouth wide open, filtering water for plankton and jellyfish.

name: Mimic Goatfish
description: It can mimic the coloration of the bluestripe snapper (Lutjanus kasmira) with which it is often found in schools.

name: Moorish Idol
description: Its genus name, Zanclus, comes from the Greek word for “sickle” and refers to the long sickle-shaped dorsal fin.

name: Ninja Lanternshark
description: A young cousin of the researcher who identified it suggested its common name, pointing out that “ninjas are awesome.”

name: Nurse Shark
description: Found in coral reefs and shallow waters, this bottom-feeder can generate incredible suction with its mouth.

name: Owlfish
ability: [Discard] also, if [BlueCoral][BlueCoral][BlueCoral] in this dive site: [FishEgg]
description: It is characterized by its large eyes (compared to its body size), which allow it to see in the dim light of the deep ocean.

name: Portuguese Dogfish
description: Its acute vision is optimized to detect small prey, but it has been known to take bites out of live seals and even whales.

name: Prickly Anglerfish
ability: [FishEgg] also, if [PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral] in this dive site: [DrawCard]
description: This deep-sea fish has a bioluminescent lure to attract prey, and it has spikes all over its body to help ward off predators.

name: Red-lipped Batfish
description: It “walks” along the sandy bottom of a reef or the ocean floor. Its bright red mouth has the appearance of wearing lipstick.

name: Reef Triggerfish
ability: [FishFromHand][ArrowDown][AnyCoral][AnyCoral][AnyCoral] (in a dive site with at least 3)
description: Called humuhumunukunukuapua'a in Hawaiian (or just humuhumu for short), it is the state fish of Hawaii.

name: Ribbon Sawtail Fish
ability: [FishHatch] also, if [GreenCoral][GreenCoral][GreenCoral] in this dive site: [DrawCard]
description: It has photophores along its body and a bioluminescent lure. The larval form has eyes on long, periscopic stalks.

name: Ribboned Sweetlips
description: As its common name suggests, this fish has fleshy lips. The inside of its mouth is bright red, and its chin is white.

name: Rose-veiled Fairy Wrasse
description: When seen in the dark, it appears deep purple with gleaming pink around its head and throughout its fins.

name: Rough Abyssal Grenadier
ability: [FishHatch] also, if [GreenCoral][GreenCoral][GreenCoral] in this dive site: [FishFromHandConsume]
description: It has an oxygen-filled swim bladder, rare for deep-sea fish, for bouyancy at the extreme depths it inhabits.

name: Roving Coral Grouper
description: It is known to occasionally engage in cooperative hunting, teaming up with the giant moray (Gymnothorax javanicus).

name: Sablefish
ability: [FishEgg] also, if [PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral] in this dive site: [FishHatch]
description: The sablefish spawns multiple times in a season, and it has a maximum recorded age of 94 years.

name: Sand Tiger Shark
description: Not a close relative of the tiger shark, it is the only shark known to store air in its body to maintain neutral buoyancy.

name: Sarcastic Fringehead
description: Males “wrestle” to establish territorial dominance, pressing their distended mouths against each other.

name: Shortfin Mako
description: One of only five species of sharks known to have warm blood, several degrees warmer than the surrounding water.

name: Shortnose Demon Catshark
description: Most deepwater catsharks lay egg cases with long tendrils at the corners that help attach them to the seafloor.

name: Sixgill Sawshark
description: It hunts using its two sensitive barbels and electric sense, incapacitating prey with a rostrum that is edged with teeth.

name: Spotted Scat
description: This fish can sting with dorsal and ventral fin spines, inflicting a venom that causes great pain and dizziness for humans.

name: Spotted Wobbegong
description: They sometimes gather in distinct social groups, not randomly or according to the amount of food available.

name: Starry Pipefish
description: Like the seahorses they resemble, female pipefish deposit partial clutches into the male's brood pouch to gestate.

name: Stellate Puffer
description: In addition to being able to inflate its body, it protects itself by ingesting toxic bacteria throughout its life.

name: Striated Frogfish
ability: [FishEgg] also, if [PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral] in this dive site: [Discard]
description: Called “hairy” for its soft spines, it has the fastest known fish bite (1/6000th of a second), swallowing prey whole.

name: Striped Eel Catfish
description: The first dorsal and each of the pectoral fins have a highly venomous spine. Wounds may even be fatal to humans.

name: Swell Shark
description: As a defense mechanism, this catshark is able to expand its body to twice its regular size by swallowing water.

name: Sydney's Pygmy Pipehorse
description: Seahorses and pipefishes are very similar. There are also a few known species of pipehorses, which have attributes of both.

name: Tasseled Scorpionfish
ability: 5 [Wave] if [AnyCoral][AnyCoral][AnyCoral] in ALL your dive sites
description: The largest species in its genus, this ambush predator has vibrant markings that camouflage it between rocky reefs.

name: Tiger Shark
description: The tiger shark is a solitary, nocturnal hunter. It has the widest range of prey among all sharks.

name: Tomato Clownfish
description: A mucous coating lets it shelter inside an anemone, and, in turn, the anemone defends its home from parasites and predators.

name: Toothy Goby
description: Its sucker-like pelvic fins allow the toothy goby to attach itself to soft corals (such as bubble coral) and sponges.

name: Variegated Lizardfish
ability: 3 [Wave] if [AnyCoral][AnyCoral][AnyCoral][AnyCoral][AnyCoral] (in a dive site with at least 5)
description: An ambush predator, its vibrant markings camouflage it between rocky reefs where it waits, scanning for prey.

name: Velvet Belly Lanternshark
description: Its black underside is not bioluminescent and is sharply delineated from the rest of its body like a patch of velvet.

name: Yellow Clown Goby
description: Unlike most gobies, which are burrowers, these roost in groups of up to 15 in the outer branches of staghorn corals.

name: Yokozuna Slickhead
ability: [FishFromHand][ArrowDown][AnyCoral][AnyCoral][AnyCoral][AnyCoral][AnyCoral] (in a dive site with at least 5)
description: “Yokozuna” is the highest rank of sumo wrestler. This slickhead is a top predator in Japan's abyssal Suruga Bay.

name: Zebra Seahorse
description: Seahorses are famously ovoviviparous. The male carries the fertilized eggs in a brood pouch situated under the tail.

name: African Coelacanth
description: Its oldest known fossils date back 410 million years. Once thought extinct, “the living fossil” was discovered in 1938.

name: Atlantic Barracudina
ability: [FishHatch] also, if [GreenCoral][GreenCoral][GreenCoral] in this dive site: [SchoolFeederMove]
description: It has a luminescent duct on its ventral surface to help it camouflage through countershading.

name: Blue Antimora
ability: [Discard] also, if [BlueCoral][BlueCoral][BlueCoral] in this dive site: [FishFromHandConsume]
description: It has small teeth located on the roof of the mouth to grip invertebrates before swallowing them.

name: Fanfin Anglerfish
ability: [FishEgg] also, if [PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral][PurpleCoral] in this dive site: [GreenCoral]
description: Recent footage has shown that its fin rays and filaments appear to emit light, in addition to its bioluminescent lure.

name: Illuminated Netdevil
description: It has a multi-branched bioluminescent barbel hanging from its lower jaw and a bioluminescent lure on its head.
